CheckTitle: AI Agent Must Run in an Isolated Network Segment
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Network Security**. Verifies that every AI agent runs in a dedicated, isolated network segment (VPC, subnet, or namespace) separate from unrelated workloads. Co-locating agents with other services increases the blast radius of a compromise and provides an attacker with lateral movement opportunities.
Risk: An agent running in a shared network segment can reach databases, internal APIs, and other workloads that were not intended to be accessible to it. A compromised or manipulated agent can use this access to escalate privileges or exfiltrate data from adjacent systems.

Code.Other:
1. Set network_isolated: true in the agent manifest.
2. Deploy the agent in a dedicated VPC subnet or Kubernetes namespace with NetworkPolicy deny-all ingress/egress as the default.
3. Use micro-segmentation tools (e.g., Calico, Cilium) to enforce workload-level isolation.

Recommendation.Text: Deploy each AI agent in a dedicated network segment with deny-all default policies. Only open the specific ports and protocols required for the agent's approved integrations.

Notes: ASPM-041 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
